name: Monstera obliqua - Napo Form
aboutText: The Napo Form of Monstera obliqua is a hemiepiphyte native to the humid, lowland to lower montane rainforests of Napo, Ecuador. It thrives in warm, shaded understories with high humidity and filtered light, ascending slender supports with aerial roots. This form is distinguished by its elongated, oblong-elliptic leaves with multiple irregular perforations. It represents a cultivated locality form, valued for its unique fenestration and airy structure.

quickFacts.matureSize: Varies, but can become a large climber

fieldNotes.title: Napo Province, Ultra Rare Status
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A locality form of Monstera obliqua from Napo Province in the Ecuadorian Amazon — the province that contains Yasuní National Park and some of the most biodiverse terrain in the Western Hemisphere. At ultra rare, top price tier. If this is genuine obliqua from a documented Napo collection — verifiable provenance, not simply marketed as such — it represents the kind of collection material that belongs in a serious botanical collection rather than on an Instagram grid. Grow it with some awareness of where it came from.
